result:
I would make every human mind incapable of sustaining the illusion that anyone’s suffering is "not my concern."  

Not by forcing empathy, but by dissolving the mental walls that let us partition the world into “people like me” and the rest. One change: the moment you grasp that another being is in pain, the same alarm bell that rings for your own pain keeps ringing until the pain is relieved. No off switch, no mute button, no philosophical loopholes.  

The world would still be complex—resources scarce, conflicts real, trade-offs inevitable—but the category of “acceptable collateral damage” would simply cease to exist. Every policy, every product, every border, every bedtime story would have to pass the test of: does this increase or decrease the pain I can’t unknow?  

That single edit to our cognitive source code would rewrite economics, governance, technology, art, even love, faster than any law or revolution could.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
